steps.title: Graph each choice
steps.content: A quadratic equation has no real solutions when its graph has no x-intercepts. In Desmos, graph the left side of each answer choice as a function of $x$ and look for the one that never touches the x-axis.

steps.title: Check the discriminant for each choice
steps.content: Choices A ($-2x^{2}=0$), B ($-2x^{2}+2=0$), and C ($-2x^{2}+2x=0$) all hit the x-axis, so they have real solutions. Choice D, $-2x^2+2x-2=0$, stays below the x-axis. Algebra confirms it: the discriminant is $2^2-4(-2)(-2)=4-16=-12\lt 0$, so there are no real solutions. D is correct.
